Malaria.notes: Requires prompt antimalarial medication and a complete course.

Jaundice.notes: Treatment focuses on the underlying cause; consult a specialist.

Typhoid.notes: Strong antibiotics required. Crucial to complete the course.

Dengue.notes: Pain relief is key. Avoid Aspirin and NSAIDs.

Hyperthyroidism.notes: Anti-thyroid medication to reduce hormone production.

Food Poisoning.notes: Focus on fluid and electrolyte replacement.

GERD.notes: Proton Pump Inhibitor (PPI) to reduce stomach acid.

Bronchitis.notes: Symptomatic treatment; antibiotics only if bacterial.

Migraine.notes: Specific migraine medications for acute attacks.

Piles.notes: Focus on reducing inflammation and easing bowel movements.

Psoriasis.drug: Topical Steroids (Betamethasone) or Vitamin D analogues
Psoriasis.notes: Requires topical treatment and, sometimes, systemic therapy.

Chronic Fatigue Syndrome.notes: No single cure; focus on rest, diet, and pain relief.

Appendicitis.notes: Requires IMMEDIATE surgical evaluation and removal.

Diabetes.notes: Blood sugar regulation medication and dietary changes.

Anemia.notes: Treatment depends on the type of anemia; often nutrient replacement.
